item.title: Learn Ruby: 5. Object-Oriented Programming
item.author: zvlex

item.title: Learn Ruby: 4. Bundler, Gems, Pry, RegExp, and More
item.author: zvlex
item.description:
Bundler Bundler is Ruby's dependency manager — it lets you install, update, and manage gems in your project.
bundle init Let's write a small program that calls the GitHub API and prints the response. We'll use the http and pry libraries.
Create a lecture4 folder and run:
bundle init This generates a Gemfile:
# frozen_string_literal: true
source "https://rubygems.org"
# gem "rails" Any gem you want to use must be declared in this file....

item.title: Learn Ruby: 3. Blocks, Iterators, and Files
item.author: zvlex
item.description:
Blocks A block is simply Ruby code enclosed between do and end:
['Ruby', 'Elixir', 'Dart'].each do |lang| puts "Learning {lang}" end
=> Learning Ruby Learning Elixir Learning Dart
If it fits on one line, you can use curly braces {}:
['Ruby', 'Elixir', 'Dart'].each { |lang| puts "Learning {lang}" }
yield yield is a special Ruby operator that executes the code passed as a block to a method:
def execute_block puts 'Start execution' yield ...

item.title: Learn Ruby: 2. Basics
item.author: zvlex
item.description:
Let's explore fundamental Ruby concepts through interactive console examples using irb.
Classes and Initialization class Terminal attr_accessor :title
def initialize(title = 'Awesome Term') @title = title end
def run(command = nil) puts "Welcome to {title}!" puts "Running your '{command}' command..." if command end end Key concepts: instance variables (prefixed with @), the initialize method, and require_relative for loading external files.
...

item.title: Learn Ruby: 1. Installing Ruby with asdf on Fedora Linux
item.author: zvlex
item.description:
Initial Setup Start by installing development tools and utilities:
sudo dnf -y groupinstall "Development Tools" sudo dnf install vim git curl wget zsh util-linux-user
For those who prefer zsh with Oh My Zsh:
sudo dnf install zsh util-linux-user sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"
Note: If you change a command language interpreter from bash to zsh, you need to restart your system.
Installing asdf and ...

item.title: Learn Ruby: Intro
item.author: zvlex
item.description:
Learn Ruby is an introductory course designed for beginners who want to learn Ruby programming from scratch, requiring only basic Unix/Linux knowledge.
Course Contents 1. Installing Ruby with asdf on Fedora Linux
2. Ruby basics
3. Blocks, Iterators, and Files
About RubyRuby is a dynamic, general-purpose programming language with elegant syntax, mostly used to build complex web applications.
The language emerged in Japan in 1995, created by Yukihiro Matsumoto (known as Matz). His ph...
